$comment: Generative news-article corpus for the 1980s (brash, punchy sports-desk voice shaped by television, wire copy, back pages, pit boards and workshop debriefs), parsed by Companion.Core.News.NewsArticleBank. Bodies under 'race.result|cause' compose fact tokens and {pool:name} references into 'pools'. Copy remains valid for both turbocharged and naturally aspirated entries. Allowed fact tokens: {player} {team} {race} {year} {round} {position} {expected} {winner} {fieldSize} {champPosition} {champMove} {champLeader}. Add eras/events by adding JSON only.

1980s: TRACKSIDE AT {race}, {year}. The wire's red-hot.
SPORTS DESK, {race}, round {round}.
LIVE FROM {race}: the result is through.
FROM THE PIT WALL, {race}, {year}.
PADDOCK REPORT, {race}, and the classification is posted.
HOT OFF THE WIRE, {race}, {year}.
GRANDSTAND VIEW, {race}: round {round} is complete.
TELETYPE FROM {race}, the {year} result is in.
PRESS ROOM FEED, {race}: round {round}.
PRESS ROOM, {race}, {year}, typewriters hammering, deadline looming.
CHEQUERED FLAG AT {race}, round {round} is in the books.
The classification is posted and {team} has a result to study.
Another line goes into the {year} record.
The transporters move on; the timing sheet stays.
The paddock turns from headlines to the next round.
The team leaves with a completed race and a page of notes.
The result gives {team} a baseline for what comes next.
Back-page deadlines pass; the championship keeps moving.
The final order will travel with the team to the next debrief.
The flag settles the argument; the record keeps the result.
For {team}, the next job starts with this classification.
The tape is filed and the season rolls on.
Back to the drawing board, and fast.
{team} has a difficult result to pick apart.
The garage turns to the debrief and the next round.
No highlight reel can change the classification.
The questions travel home with {team}.
Monday starts with the timing sheet and a long list.
The result is final; the response begins in the workshop.
The paddock moves on, but {team} has work to do.
Another round, another hard set of notes for the crew.
The debrief will be direct and the response plan deliberate.
The page is filed; the lessons still need writing.
In the title race {player} sits {champPosition}, {champMove}.
The championship table now reads {player} {champPosition}, {champMove} on the year.
{player} climbs to {champPosition} in the points, {champMove}.
That's {player} {champPosition} in the standings, {champMove}.
Over on the title sheet {player} holds {champPosition}, {champMove}.
The points board says {player} {champPosition} now, {champMove} on the season.
That shuffles the standings: {player} {champPosition}, {champMove}.
In the championship hunt {player} is {champPosition}, {champMove} after that one.
The title arithmetic leaves {player} {champPosition}, {champMove}.
default: That leaves {player} {champPosition} in the title race.

1980s: against a form guide that had {expected} pencilled in
when the paddock preview called {expected}
with the paperwork pointing to {expected}
against a pre-race estimate of {expected}
when the smart money had them down for {expected}
with the sports desks predicting {expected}
against a forecast of {expected}
with the form book insisting on {expected}
though every preview had forecast {expected}
default: clear of the form-book mark at {expected}

1980s: The result, not the rhetoric, did the talking.
The stopwatch gave {team} its answer.
Race pace translated into a place on the sheet.
The machine and driver delivered the recorded result.
The timing sheet carries the proof.
The car had enough for the result it earned.
The final classification settles the performance.
The pace became a number in the record.
The flag turned the afternoon into a result.
The {team} entry reached the line with its place confirmed.
Whatever the noise, the order is now official.
The cameras caught the flag and the final order.
Press-room typewriters started before the cars stopped.
The result headed straight for the evening bulletins.
The gantry cameras stayed on the finish.
The wire services carried the classification.
The back-page desks had their headline.
The television crews turned from track to paddock.
The final order moved from timing sheet to newsroom.
The press room had its story for the night.
The photographs and classification went out together.
The transporters roll home and the winter work begins for {team}.
The final table is filed; attention turns to the next campaign.
The cameras are packed away and the workshops take over.
The {year} record is complete, with another season waiting beyond winter.
Trophies and timing sheets go into the cabinet; the planning starts again.
The last deadlines pass and {team} turns to the off-season.
The championship book closes; the next design brief opens.
The year is done, and the paddock's work moves behind closed doors.
default: The books close on another year.

1980s: Typewriters hammered in the press room as the result came through.
Pit boards and timing sheets carried the final order along the lane.
The television crews had their pictures before the transporters were packed.
Across the paddock, team managers studied the classification.
Mechanics gathered around the timing sheet after the flag.
The result moved quickly from pit wall to press room.
Back-page deadlines waited for nobody once the order was official.
The cameras turned away only after the final classification was posted.
default: The result circulated through the paddock.
The report moved on to the next round.

1980s: Back in the garage, the crew began a methodical inspection.
The car returned behind the screens for the mechanics to diagnose.
Tools came out as soon as the machine reached the {team} garage.
The engineers recorded the symptoms and turned to the strip-down.
A long evening of checks waited behind the pit shutters.
The mechanics gathered around the car and started with the basics.
The transporter would carry a full repair list back to the factory.
The garage changed from race mode to diagnosis without delay.
default: The repair list started to grow.
The garage turned to the work ahead.
